tasks.objective: Implement `lumyn init` and `lumyn check` for local OpenAPI/docs source intake and workflow-relevant source findings.
acceptance_checks: `lumyn init` writes valid config.
`lumyn check` emits concrete source references.
RCRR-001: `lumyn init` writes valid config and source-check artifacts.
RCRR-002: `lumyn check` emits a workflow-relevant source finding with a concrete reference.
ACT-002: `init` plus `check` completed in under 10 minutes.
FR1: FR1: Product Surface Under Test - Lumyn must frame the customer's API/docs surface as the system under test and agents as probes.
FR2: FR2: Local-First Operation - The MVP must work without a hosted Lumyn account.
FR3: FR3: OpenAPI And Local Docs Intake - Lumyn must ingest an OpenAPI file and local docs folder.
FR4: FR4: Agent-Relevant Source Checks - Lumyn must run structural checks that affect agent tool choice, validation, auth handling, retries, and workflow proof.
NFR1: NFR1: Fast First Value - A technical user should run `init`, `check`, and open bundled traces in under 10 minutes.
NFR8: NFR8: Parser-First - OpenAPI checks should use structured parsers and schema-aware libraries where possible.

architecture_guidance_refs.rule: Medium/high risk tasks record state owner, feedback source, blast radius, source-of-truth impact, and rollback/deletion checks.
Behavior and schema/output changes use test-first evidence or a structured skipped reason.
Boundary, public contract, failure semantics, credential/network, release, or major reliability/perf changes require ADR or decision note.
Fan-out, repeated runs, or model calls declare expected cost/performance impact.
Retry, cleanup, persistence, filesystem mutation, redaction, live network, and provider code require hardening/failure-mode evidence.
Deterministic bootstrap has no network/secrets; live work requires approval and evidence.

engineering_policy_refs.rule: Behavior, flags, output shape, exit codes, artifact paths, install paths, and workflow semantics update docs and Factory planning artifacts together.

tasks.objective: Implement record/redact/normalize pipeline and draft workflow/cassette artifacts for a deterministic mock API.
acceptance_checks: Standard auth headers are redacted before persistence.
State bindings and validator drafts are generated with confidence labels.
RCRR-003: `lumyn record create_customer_with_readback` writes workflow YAML, cassette, trace, and state bindings.
RCRR-004: Generated workflow YAML is readable and editable without a custom UI.
RCRR-009: Standard auth headers and configured sensitive fields are redacted in all artifacts.
ACT-003: First workflow recorded and replay-verified in under 30 minutes.
ACT-004: Generated workflow contract accepted with limited edits.
REC-QUALITY-001: At least 70% of drafted assertions, read-back candidates, and state bindings are accepted without edit on simple REST/OpenAPI workflows.
FR5: FR5: Workflow Contract Schema - Lumyn must define a versioned YAML workflow contract with goal, expected outcome, validators, state bindings, context, constraints, and cleanup.
FR6: FR6: Recording As Authoring - Lumyn must record known-good HTTP traffic and draft workflow contracts, validators, state bindings, cassettes, and cleanup suggestions.
FR7: FR7: User Approval - Generated workflow contracts must be editable and approved before being treated as stable.
FR8: FR8: Capture-Time Redaction - Secrets must be redacted before requests, responses, cassettes, traces, or reports are persisted.
NFR2: NFR2: Fast First Workflow - A technical user should record and replay-verify one simple workflow in under 30 minutes.

NFR5: NFR5: Security - Redaction must happen before artifact persistence. If a value cannot be confidently redacted, Lumyn must fail closed: mark the artifact unsafe to persist rather than write a possibly-unredacted secret. Silent persistence of an unrecognized secret is a trust bug, not an edge case.
NFR7: NFR7: Contract Readability - Workflow YAML must be understandable by engineers without a custom UI.
NFR13: NFR13: Inference Humility - Recorder-generated validators, bindings, boundaries, and cleanup suggestions must be confidence-labeled.
FR2: FR2: Local-First Operation - The MVP must work without a hosted Lumyn account.
FR9: FR9: Canonical Evidence Schema - Record, replay verify, live verify, eval, validators, and reports must consume canonical evidence events.
FR14: FR14: Stable CLI Output - Lumyn must support stable exit codes and `--json`.
NFR6: NFR6: Local Privacy - Lumyn must not send specs, docs, traces, cassettes, or API responses to Lumyn by default.
NFR9: NFR9: Debuggable Errors - Errors must include file, path, command, object, or line references where possible.
NFR12: NFR12: Artifact Stability - Schemas and artifacts must be versioned.

architecture_guidance_refs.rule: Medium/high risk tasks record state owner, feedback source, blast radius, source-of-truth impact, and rollback/deletion checks.

Boundary, public contract, failure semantics, credential/network, release, or major reliability/perf changes require ADR or decision note.
Fan-out, repeated runs, or model calls declare expected cost/performance impact.
Retry, cleanup, persistence, filesystem mutation, redaction, live network, and provider code require hardening/failure-mode evidence.
Deterministic bootstrap has no network/secrets; live work requires approval and evidence.

engineering_policy_refs.rule: Behavior, flags, output shape, exit codes, artifact paths, install paths, and workflow semantics update docs and Factory planning artifacts together.
Command-result JSON, exit codes, schemas, and product artifacts are public or executable contracts.
Release-sensitive work defines version, changelog, install, artifact-integrity, and UAT evidence before merge.
Validation, work-proof, PR lifecycle, scanner, and scope-closure evidence use repo-relative refs.

CI/workflow/policy/lifecycle work preserves required-check metadata, owner-review coverage, workflow permissions, concurrency, timeouts, toolchain-pin setup, action-ref posture, and branch-protection evidence or approved exceptions.

runtime_pins.dependency_policy: standard library first; pinned Go modules only when a task proves need and records justification
runtime_pins.live_work_policy: blocked until deterministic replay foundation passes and human approval unlocks sandbox/model/network posture
runtime_pins.provider_policy: OpenAI-compatible HTTP and Anthropic Messages HTTP adapters; no model key or network in deterministic bootstrap
runtime_pins.artifact_namespace: .factory/artifacts/ for Factory evidence; product artifacts use workflows/, cassettes/, runs/, baselines/, schemas/, and examples/

slice_rationale.why_this_shape: T11 is scoped to implement live eval provider interface, OpenAI-compatible and Anthropic provider adapters, stateless run defaults, context modes, repeated runs, and trace capture.
slice_rationale.vertical_slice_preference: Vertical slices are preferred; foundation-only work must justify why downstream product slices need the shared contract first.

changelog.draft_entry: Add live eval provider interface, OpenAI-compatible and Anthropic adapters, context modes, repeated runs, and trace capture.

tasks.contract_impact: Adds live eval provider interface, OpenAI-compatible and Anthropic adapters, context mode, repeated run, and trace capture contracts.
tasks.versioning_migration_impact: Pre-1.0 MVP work may evolve contracts, but introduced schema_version values, exit codes, artifact names, and command-result fields must retain explicit migration notes before release.
architecture_constraints: Preserve Go 1.26.4 and module path github.com/Clyra-AI/lumyn unless alignment artifacts are updated first.
Keep deterministic bootstrap work free of live network calls, sandbox credentials, and model keys.
Keep command-result JSON, exit codes, schemas, and persisted artifacts repo-relative and schema-backed where a schema exists.
Fail closed when proof strength, redaction confidence, credential posture, stale cassette state, or declared controls are ambiguous.
Record state owner, feedback source, deletion/blast-radius impact, rollback path, and evidence refs for medium/high risk architecture changes.
Live credential, model-key, and network work remains blocked until explicit human approval and allowlist posture are recorded.
tasks.adr_trigger_reason: Required because this task changes public, executable, CLI, schema, artifact, CI/review, redaction/share, live/eval, or proof semantics captured by the Lumyn plan.
tdd_first_failing_tests: Add or update the smallest failing test, fixture, or schema validation for T11 acceptance before implementation when practical.
If a red-first check is not practical, record test_first_skipped_reason in the validation report with compensating evidence.

cost_perf_impact.measurement_expectation: Keep deterministic commands fast and local; live/eval work must record duration, retry, and cost evidence before closure.
cost_perf_impact.budget_trigger: Add explicit performance/cost evidence if implementation introduces repeated replay, live network, model calls, generated artifacts, or large fixture processing.

chaos_failure_hypothesis.hypothesis: Provider errors, nondeterminism, missing credentials, or unsafe context modes must be labeled and kept out of default hard CI gates.
chaos_failure_hypothesis.expected_fail_closed_behavior: Return structured command-result/error evidence, do not mark acceptance green, and update validation/scope closure with the failure or blocker.

semantic_invariants: Every command path emits the stable command-result envelope, including failures.
Exit codes remain aligned with docs/product/prd.md and tests.
Validation evidence records command, exit status, and skipped-command reason when applicable.
Factory evidence and product artifacts use repo-relative paths only.
No task may claim closure for PRD scope that remains missing, blocked, or deferred without explicit evidence.
Live credential/network work is disabled by default and cannot run without approved posture evidence.
tasks.plan_drift_policy: If this task changes runtime, distribution, provider, dependency, artifact namespace, live-work, guide, validation-contract, acceptance-ledger, acceptance-mapping, or scope-closure decisions, update context brief, execution plan, task packets, validation contract, acceptance ledger, acceptance mapping, and scope-closure map before implementation continues.

deprecated_worker_aliases.migration_behavior: block active task packets until required_worker_chain is migrated

scope_exclusions: training shared models on private artifacts

mvp_eval_provider_adapters.sdk_policy: no heavy SDK dependency for MVP bootstrap; use thin HTTP adapters unless a later task justifies a pinned SDK
mvp_eval_provider_adapters.network_policy: blocked until deterministic replay foundation passes and human approval unlocks model/network posture

factoryd_runtime.approval_posture: human approval required for live credentials, high-risk tasks, and merge
factoryd_runtime.credential_posture: no ambient secrets during deterministic MVP bootstrap
factoryd_runtime.network_posture: offline by default until live sandbox/model work is approved

stop_conditions: missing runner-ready task contract
changed path outside allowed_paths
forbidden path changed
required validation command failed
plan drift detected
required lifecycle evidence missing without approved exception
credential or network access required before approval posture is unlocked
coverage policy evidence is missing or below threshold without an approved scoped exception
required-check metadata, workflow permissions, concurrency, timeouts, owner-review coverage, action-ref posture, or branch-protection evidence is missing for CI/workflow/policy/lifecycle work
acceptance ledger item missing, unmapped, or closed only at group level

coverage_policy_refs.exception_policy: coverage misses block promotion unless the task records an approved scoped exception with owner, reason, expiry or follow-up task, and compensating validation evidence

gated_by_acceptance_items.reason: Live eval work must not start until product pull shows a user recorded more than one workflow.
Live eval work must not start until product pull shows a user asked to run live eval before an API/docs release.

tasks.objective: Implement eval baseline comparison, drift labels, cost/duration reporting, and diagnostic failure classification.
acceptance_checks: Eval compares against saved baseline where configured.
Model snapshot uncertainty is labeled as drift risk.
Failures include concrete trace evidence.
EVAL-006: Eval reports cost and duration.
EVAL-007: Eval writes a local trace/report.
EVAL-008: Eval compares to a saved baseline where configured.
EVAL-009: Eval labels baseline drift risk when model snapshots are unavailable.
EVAL-010: Eval is diagnostic by default unless an explicit gate is configured.
FR22: FR22: Model Pinning - Lumyn must record provider, model, temperature, and model snapshot where available.
FR23: FR23: Baseline Comparison - Lumyn eval must compare against a saved baseline where configured and label drift risk when model snapshots are unavailable.
FR24: FR24: Least-Privilege Agent Runs - Lumyn eval must default to stateless local runs, explicit context, allowlisted env vars, and no ambient workspace access.
FR25: FR25: Portable Artifacts - Workflow contracts, cassettes, traces, reports, and baselines must remain useful outside a hosted service.
NFR10: NFR10: Cost Visibility - Live evals must show token and estimated cost information.
NFR11: NFR11: Stochastic Honesty - Eval must report run counts and pass rates. It must not hide variability behind a deterministic-looking boolean.
FR2: FR2: Local-First Operation - The MVP must work without a hosted Lumyn account.
FR9: FR9: Canonical Evidence Schema - Record, replay verify, live verify, eval, validators, and reports must consume canonical evidence events.
FR14: FR14: Stable CLI Output - Lumyn must support stable exit codes and `--json`.
NFR6: NFR6: Local Privacy - Lumyn must not send specs, docs, traces, cassettes, or API responses to Lumyn by default.
NFR9: NFR9: Debuggable Errors - Errors must include file, path, command, object, or line references where possible.
NFR12: NFR12: Artifact Stability - Schemas and artifacts must be versioned.
